---
title: "MLSys Playground"
subtitle: "Feel the constraint. Small games that make ML systems playable."
description: "Browser mini-games that teach ML systems concepts through play. Each game isolates one physical law of ML — memory, compute, precision — and makes it a reflex before a formula."
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/games/common.css">
---

Every game here isolates one real ML systems concept — the kind that determines whether a model ships — and turns it into a 30-second loop. The idea: your hands learn the constraint before your head names it.

::: {.mlsp-game-cards}

::: {.mlsp-game-card}
### Prune
[Most weights don't matter. Find which ones do.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Click faint connections in a neural network to remove them. Keep accuracy above the threshold. Teaches magnitude-based pruning and the lottery-ticket hypothesis.

[▶ Play Prune](/games/prune.html){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

::: {.mlsp-game-card .mlsp-coming-soon}
### Roofline Runner
[Catch kernels under the ceiling.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Position your arithmetic intensity to catch incoming kernels (GEMM, attention, elementwise) under the roofline ceiling. Teaches memory-bound vs compute-bound and the ridge point.

[Coming soon](#){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

::: {.mlsp-game-card .mlsp-coming-soon}
### OOM
[Survive the forward + backward pass.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Place activation and gradient blocks into a bounded GPU memory region. Decide what to checkpoint. Teaches the memory hierarchy of training and recompute tradeoffs.

[Coming soon](#){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

:::

---

*MLSys Playground is an experiment in teaching systems through play. Games share the same design vocabulary as the [ML Systems textbook](/) — compute blue, data green, error red — so a figure in the book and a game in your browser feel like the same world.*
